title: Phanera esquirolii
taxonomy: Phanera esquirolii belongs to the family Apocynaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is a climbing plant native to Asia and is also known as the Chinese milk vine. The classification of Phanera esquirolii is further categorized under the genus Phanera. This genus is a member of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae. Phanera esquirolii is a rare and unique species that is often used in traditional medicine.

intro: Phanera esquirolii, also known as Esqin, is a plant species belonging to the family Moraceae, commonly found in tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Phanera esquirolii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Phanera esquirolii is used to prepare various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are consumed to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
